title: Password-change template path is inconsistent by case
reasoning: PasswordChangeView renders "Users/password_change.html" for GET and old-password errors, but renders "users/password_change.html" for other invalid form errors. On case-sensitive deployments, one of these paths will not resolve unless both template directories exist, causing TemplateDoesNotExist on some password-change flows.
reproduction: On a case-sensitive filesystem with only the lowercase users template path, request the password-change GET page or submit a wrong old password.
recommendation: Use one canonical template path consistently, preferably matching the lowercase paths used by the rest of the views, and ensure the template exists at that path.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Users/tests.py has no tests that render PasswordChangeView for GET, wrong old password, or mismatched new passwords.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add tests for PasswordChangeView GET and invalid POST cases that assert a 200 response and the expected template.
minimumFixScope: Normalize the two uppercase template references in PasswordChangeView and add focused view tests.
